gitCommit.subject: WEBDEV-1475: drop networkidle wait from all visual specs
gitCommit.body:
WEBDEV-1475: drop networkidle wait from all visual specs

`page.waitForLoadState('networkidle', { timeout: 10_000 }).catch(() => {})`
was always timing out on production pages — Vimeo embeds, Marketo
forms, GTM/Bing UET trackers etc. keep the network constantly busy,
so the page never reaches network idle within 10s. Failure was
swallowed by .catch but every snapshot still lost 10s wall time.

Page is already settled enough for screenshotting by:
  - `waitUntil: 'load'` in gotoOrSkip()
  - stabilizePage() (animations off + reduced-motion)
  - `root.waitFor({ state: 'visible' })` on the element being snapped

Removed the line from all 15 visual specs. Saves ~10s × 15 ≈ 2.5 min
on Phase 3 runtime; per-spec individual wait drops from ~14s to ~4s
in the trace timeline.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

errors.message:
Cannot use({ trace }) in a describe group, because it forces a new worker.
Make it top-level in the test file or put in the configuration file.
errors.snippet:
at nav-pages-sanity.spec.ts:112

[0m [90m 110 |[39m   [90m// 100MB-per-file push limit. Per-page screenshots in screenshots/nav-sanity/[39m
 [90m 111 |[39m   [90m// are still captured for visible evidence.[39m
[31m[1m>[22m[39m[90m 112 |[39m   test[33m.[39muse({ trace[33m:[39m [32m'off'[39m[33m,[39m video[33m:[39m [32m'off'[39m })[33m;[39m
 [90m     |[39m        [31m[1m^[22m[39m
 [90m 113 |[39m
 [90m 114 |[39m   [36mlet[39m navPaths[33m:[39m string[] [33m=[39m [][33m;[39m
 [90m 115 |[39m[0m

errors.message:
Cannot use({ video }) in a describe group, because it forces a new worker.
Make it top-level in the test file or put in the configuration file.
